responses: Hello! I am your CampusAI Assistant. How can I help you today?
Good to see you again! Feel free to ask me anything about MNU.
Hi there, how can I help you navigate Mama Ngina University?

text: Tell me about Mama Ngina University
responses: Mama Ngina University College (MNUC) is a constituent college of Kenyatta University, initially mentored by Moi University. It is located in Gatundu, Kiambu County.
Vision: A Premier University that nurtures Competent and Value-based leaders. Mission: To provide transformative teaching, research, and community engagement. Motto: Shaping the Future.
General Inquiry: 020-208-4004 or info@mnu.ac.ke. Admissions: admissions@mnu.ac.ke. Registrar (ASA): +254 748 868 921.
Fees are payable to: Equity Bank (A/C: 0660282250603) or Co-operative Bank (A/C: 01129556920900). First-semester fee for new students is approx KES 34,700.

text: what documents do i need for registration?
responses: Required: Original admission letter, original and copies of academic certificates, and National ID. A satisfactory Medical Report (Form MNU/4) is mandatory.
Semester 2 for 2026 begins with reporting on 6th January 2026. Lectures commence shortly after registration.

text: is the library open on sunday?
responses: The library is open Mon-Fri (8am-6pm) and Sat (9am-6pm). Closed on Sundays. Access e-resources at library.mnu.ac.ke.
Visit support.mnu.ac.ke for portal tickets or email ictsupport@mnu.ac.ke for email and 2FA issues. WiFi setup guides for Eduroam are on the support portal.
MNU has an MOU with Qejani Hostels for safe student accommodation near campus. Students are responsible for their own meal arrangements.
Active clubs include Mentorship, Drama, Computer Science, and spiritual groups like the Christian Union and Muslim Association.
Graduands must fill the 'Academic Gowns Hiring Form'. Failure to return regalia on time incurs a KES 500 daily penalty.
Schools include: 1. Business, Humanities and Economics, 2. Pure and Applied Sciences, and 3. Health Sciences.
Goodbye! Have a great day at Mama Ngina University.
Talk to you later!
Come back soon!

text: How to apply for student aid?
responses: The MNUC Student Aid Programme supports students facing personal or financial hardship with basic necessities like food, toiletries, and clothing. Apply by downloading the form from the Student Affairs website and submitting it to head-studentaffairs@mnu.ac.ke.

text: carrying a phone in exam room
responses: Serious offences include possession of unauthorized materials (phones, notes), plagiarism, and impersonation. Involvement in any examination irregularity automatically leads to expulsion from the University College.
Students can access ODeL online classes via the Learning Management System (LMS). Detailed video and PDF guides for students are available on the main MNU website under Online Learning Resources.

text: extra time in exams for disability
responses: MNU is committed to inclusivity, providing ramps, tactile lifts, and accessible websites. Students with disabilities are allocated an extra 30 minutes in examinations and have access to mentorship services.

text: What to do in an emergency?
responses: In case of emergency, contact the Security Hotline at +254 741 016 799 or email security@mnu.ac.ke. Emergency notifications are also broadcasted via the university website and student email.
Complaints and compliments can be submitted via email to feedback@mnu.ac.ke. Physically, you can visit the Administration Building, Room 207, to fill out the official form.
Students can apply for HELB loans and scholarships through the official HELB mobile app or website. For assistance, students can visit Huduma Centres or the HELB Student Service Centre.

SAMNUC elections are governed by a strict Electoral Code. Offences include use of abusive language, defacing posters, or enlisting goons. Breaching this code can lead to disqualification and disciplinary action by the University College Student Disciplinary Committee.

text: how to apply for a job at the university
responses: All career opportunities and job vacancies are posted on the 'Vacancies' section of the official MNU website. Applications should typically be sent to the Office of the Principal or via vacancies@mnu.ac.ke as specified in the advertisement.

Interested bidders can find active tender notices on the university website under the 'Tenders' tab. Completed documents are usually submitted to the tender box at the Administration Block or via tenders@mnu.ac.ke.

The MNU Alumni Association focuses on maintaining a lifelong connection between the university and its graduates. You can register as an alumnus upon graduation to access networking events and mentorship opportunities by contacting alumni@mnu.ac.ke.

The University provides confidential counseling services through the Office of the Dean of Students. Students can walk into the Student Affairs office or book an appointment via email for support with academic, social, or personal challenges.

The university provides transport services for official academic trips and organized student events. Daily shuttle schedules for staff and students between the campus and Gatundu town are usually posted on the notice boards.

Students are expected to adhere to the MNU Student Code of Conduct, which prohibits drug abuse, violence, and unauthorized demonstrations. Breaches are handled by the Student Disciplinary Committee and can lead to suspension or expulsion.

MNU promotes research through its Research, Innovation, and Outreach (RIO) office. Academic staff and students' research works are hosted on the MNU Institutional Repository (e-space) for global visibility.
Commonly used forms such as the Admission Medical Form (MNU/4), Deferment forms, and Clearance forms are available for download in the 'Downloads' section of the university website.
Stay updated by checking the 'Latest News' and 'Announcements' sections on the university home page. Recent updates include ISO certification progress and professional academy launches.

text: Who is the Chairman of the Council?
responses: Mama Ngina University College is governed by a University Council chaired by Dr. Mutuku Matingi. The management is led by the Principal, Prof. Zipporah Ng'ang'a, supported by the Deputy Principal (ASA).

MNU is committed to environmental sustainability through its 'Green Campus' initiative. This includes regular tree-planting events, sustainable waste management, and the establishment of the Center for Climate Change and Environmental Studies.

MNU maintains a zero-tolerance policy towards sexual harassment and gender-based violence. The Gender Mainstreaming Committee ensures equal opportunities and provides a secure reporting mechanism via the Gender Desk in the Student Affairs office.

As a constituent college of Kenyatta University (KU), MNU benefits from KU’s academic mentorship and quality assurance frameworks. The university also maintains MOUs with various industrial partners for student attachments and internships.

MNU adheres to the Government of Kenya Performance Contracting guidelines to ensure efficiency. Our Service Delivery Charter, visible in all administrative blocks, outlines our commitment to timely and transparent service for all stakeholders.

MNU has a dedicated Corruption Prevention Committee. You can report any unethical behavior or bribery anonymously through the corruption reporting boxes placed around campus or via integrity@mnu.ac.ke.

All students are required to have a valid NHIF cover or an equivalent medical insurance. This ensures access to both inpatient services at recognized hospitals and outpatient care at the University Health Unit.

text: what is the name of principal
responses: Prof Zipporah Ng'ang'a is the university college principal and if you need any help then you can reach out the Head Student Affairs first.
Students will be notified the relevant university details via the student corporate emails. Keep an eye at your emails not to miss important information.

text: do we have to wear uniform
responses: Students are encouraged to dress decently to ensure a healthy environment while in class

responses: Academic calender is given to you by your class-soordinators after you join your respective classes

text: please provide sports and games information
responses: Our university encourages all-round development of students and hence provides sports facilities in the campus. For more details visit the sports office at the lecture theatre

Deans differ for each branch, please be more specific like: (dean SPAS, SBEH, SHS)

text: not seeing a grade for a unit I sat for
responses: An 'I' (Incomplete) means a grade wasn't finalized. You must fill out a Missing Marks form at the Registrar (ASA) office to have it cleared before the next semester.
Don't ignore missing marks! Contact your lecturer or the HOD immediately to ensure your scripts are verified and the correct grade is uploaded to the portal.

text: how to get on the Dean's List
what is the cut-off for honors?
responses: Academic excellence is celebrated! To make the Dean's List, you typically need a semester GPA of 3.7 or higher. This recognition is a great addition to your CV.
Students with exceptional performance are often eligible for internal scholarships or mentorship opportunities. Keep aiming for that First Class!.

text: moving my units to a different degree
can I skip some units if I have a certificate?
responses: Credit transfers are possible for students coming from recognized institutions. You must apply through the Registrar (ASA) with your official transcripts for evaluation.
Diploma holders may be eligible for exemptions in specific first-year units. Submit your application early in the semester to the Admissions office for processing.

text: how to check if I cleared all units
responses: A graduation audit ensures you've met all requirements. Visit the Registrar (ASA) office during your final year to verify your transcript and clear any pending issues.
Ensure you have no 'F' grades or 'I' grades in your record. You must also have met the minimum number of credit hours for your specific degree program.

text: requesting a remark of an exam
I am unhappy with my results
responses: You have a right to appeal if you believe a grade is incorrect. This involves a formal application to the Registrar (ASA) and paying a prescribed remarking fee.
Appeals must be done within a specific timeframe after results are released. Talk to your HOD first to see if the issue can be resolved departmentally.

text: how to create a study timetable
responses: Effective time management is a skill! Use a planner to track deadlines and try the Eisenhower Matrix to prioritize tasks. You can also get tips from the Mentorship Club.
Start assignments early to avoid last-minute stress. Break large projects into smaller tasks and set specific goals for each study session.
Try the Cornell Method: divide your page into notes, cues, and a summary. This makes reviewing much faster! Check the library for study skills resources.
Visual learners often benefit from mind mapping. Use different colors to link concepts. Experiment with both digital tools and traditional notebooks to see what fits you best.

text: how to search the library catalog
responses: The MNU Library provides access to various e-resources. Visit library.mnu.ac.ke to use the OPAC (Online Public Access Catalog) and search for specific journals.
For credible research, always look for peer-reviewed articles. The library staff offers training on information literacy to help you master these tools.

text: how to give a good presentation
responses: Confidence comes with practice! Keep your slides simple—use more visuals and less text. Join the Drama Club to practice your public speaking skills in a safe environment.
Engage your audience by making eye contact and asking questions. If you're nervous, try practicing in front of a mirror or with a small group of friends first.
Academic integrity is non-negotiable. Always cite your sources! The university uses specific referencing styles like APA. Plagiarism can lead to expulsion.
Using AI to generate entire assignments is considered an exam offence. Use it only for brainstorming or research, and always acknowledge its use as per your department's guidelines.

Sleep is vital for cognitive function. Try to maintain a consistent sleep schedule and avoid screens 30 minutes before bed. If your insomnia is linked to persistent anxiety, please visit the University Health Unit for a check-up.
Chronic tiredness can affect your grades. Speak to a counselor at the Student Affairs office to discuss stress management techniques that can help improve your rest.

text: how to get a student ID
responses: New students are issued ID cards upon completion of registration. You must have your original admission letter and provide four 1-inch studio-quality photos to the Admissions Office for processing.

All lost and found items should be reported or handed over to the University Security Office located at the main gate or the Student Affairs office. Please carry your student ID for verification when claiming an item.

To defer your studies, you must fill out a 'Request for Deferment' form available at the Registrar (ASA) office. Deferment is typically granted for one academic year and must be approved before the semester exams begin.

text: when do we go for attachment?
responses: Industrial attachment is mandatory for 3rd or 4th-year students depending on the program. You must secure a Personal Accident Insurance cover, obtain a logbook from your department, and ensure your site is approved by the university coordinator.

Unit registration is done online via the Student Portal at the start of every semester. Ensure you register for all core and elective units as per your course structure before the portal closes, usually in the second week of the semester.

text: what happens if I lose a library book?
responses: Late returns attract a daily fine of KES 5 per book. If a book is lost, the student is required to pay three times the current market value of the book plus a processing fee. All fines must be cleared before the end of the semester.

text: talking to a lecturer about my career
responses: Every student is assigned an academic mentor within their department. You can also join the MNU Mentorship Club, which facilitates peer-to-peer and professional mentorship programs throughout the semester.

text: how to get an exam card
responses: Exam cards are generated via the Student Portal once a student has cleared at least 100% of their tuition fees and registered for all units. You must print and have the card stamped at the Finance and Registrar's offices before the exam period starts.

Unofficial transcripts can be viewed and downloaded directly from the Student Portal. For an Official Transcript (with the university seal), you must apply through the Registrar (ASA) office and pay the requisite processing fee.

Internal transfers are processed in the first two weeks of the first semester. You must meet the cluster points for the new program and apply through the Registrar (ASA) using the Inter-Faculty Transfer Form.

Special exams are granted to students who miss regular exams due to medical reasons or other compassionate grounds. You must provide supporting evidence (e.g., a medical report) to the Registrar (ASA) within 48 hours.
Apply for HELB via the official HELB mobile app or website. Ensure you have a valid KRA PIN, National ID, and your parents' details ready for the application.

text: how to apply for work study
responses: The Work-Study Program supports students with financial needs by providing part-time jobs in the library or administrative offices. Applications are handled by the Dean of Students' office at the start of the semester.
To connect to 'Eduroam' WiFi, use your university corporate email as the username and your portal password. Detailed configuration guides for Android and Windows are available on the support portal.
The Learning Management System (LMS) can be accessed via lms.mnu.ac.ke. Use your Admission Number and the password provided during orientation to log in and access course materials.
MNU uses a smart card for library access and exams. If your card is lost, report it to the ICT office and pay the replacement fee at the Finance office.
MNU has active teams for Football, Darts, Athletics, and Indoor Games. Darts is particularly competitive in KUSF games. Visit the Sports Coordinator at the Dean of Students' office to join.
Spiritual groups include the Christian Union (CU), Muslim Association, SDA Association, and Catholic Students Association. They meet regularly in designated halls during non-lecture hours.
MNU has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment. You can report incidents to the Gender Desk in the Student Affairs office or via the confidential email: integrity@mnu.ac.ke.
The Mentorship Club facilitates peer counseling and mentorship. For professional support, visit the university counselor at the Student Affairs office.
Manage stress by breaking assignments into smaller tasks, taking regular breaks, and attending mentorship sessions. If pressure persists, please consult the university health unit.
Academic integrity is strictly enforced. Always cite your sources using APA format. Plagiarism or using unauthorized materials in exams can lead to immediate expulsion.
Graduands must fill out the 'Academic Gowns Hiring Form' before collection. Regalia must be returned by the specified date to avoid a daily penalty of KES 500.
Register with the MNU Alumni Association upon graduation to network with professionals and give back to the institution. Contact: alumni@mnu.ac.ke.
Report security issues to the main gate or call the Hotline: +254 741 016 799. Always carry your student ID for identification by security personnel.
The University Health Unit provides outpatient services for basic ailments. In case of serious medical emergencies, students are referred to the nearby Gatundu Level 5 Hospital.
The university canteen offers a variety of affordable meals. Students can pay for meals using cash or mobile money directly at the catering unit.

text: how to check out of hostels
responses: To check out, ensure the room is clean, return all keys to the housekeeper, and sign the official clearance form to avoid extra charges.
SAMNUC elections are held annually under a strict code of conduct. Aspiring leaders must be in good academic standing and vetted by the electoral committee.

text: switch from IT to Computer Science
responses: Inter-faculty transfers are usually processed within the first two weeks of the first semester for new students. You must meet the cluster points for the new program and apply through the Registrar (ASA) office.

For missing marks, you should fill out a 'Missing Marks Form' at your department or the Registrar (ASA) office. Ensure you have proof of sitting for the exam, such as a signed attendance list or an exam card.

text: minimum GPA to stay in school
responses: A student is placed on academic probation if their GPA falls below the university's minimum threshold (usually 2.0). If you are on probation, please visit the Dean of Students for academic advising and support.

To defer, you must submit a formal application to the Registrar (ASA) explaining your reasons. Deferment is typically granted for one academic year, and you must clear any outstanding fees before the request is approved.

text: how to register for a retake
responses: Retakes for failed units are registered during the normal unit registration period in the semester the unit is offered. A prescribed retake fee per unit is usually applicable at the finance office.
Attachment logbooks are collected from your respective department office. Ensure you have a valid Personal Accident Insurance cover before starting your industrial attachment.

text: how to apply for university bursary
responses: The university offers limited bursaries for extremely needy students. Applications are announced via the Dean of Students' office. You can also apply for CDF or County bursaries using your fee statement as proof of registration.

text: how to use NHIF at the health unit
responses: All students are required to have a valid NHIF cover. You can use your NHIF card at the University Health Unit or other accredited facilities for inpatient and outpatient services.
Academic pressure can be overwhelming. Please take a break and speak to a counselor at the Student Affairs office. Your mental health is more important than any grade. You are not alone!

Procrastination often comes from feeling overwhelmed. Try the Pomodoro technique: study for 25 minutes, then take a 5-minute break. Setting small, achievable goals each day can help you build momentum.
The university has a zero-tolerance policy for any form of harassment. Reports can be made confidentially at the Gender Desk in the Student Affairs office or to the university security office.
For ICT issues, you can raise a support ticket at the ICT helpdesk portal or visit the ICT office in the administration block for physical assistance.
You can access e-resources via the library portal using your university email credentials. This includes access to journals, e-books, and research databases like IEEE or JSTOR.

text: cost of a new student ID
responses: If you lose your ID, report to the security office and the registrar. You will need to pay a replacement fee at the finance office and provide the receipt to the ICT office for a new card.

SAMNUC is the Student Association representing your interests. Elections are held annually. You can participate by voting or running for a position if you meet the academic and disciplinary requirements.
The university motto is 'Shaping the Future.' The anthem is played at official ceremonies and reflects our commitment to excellence and transformation.

text: I lost my phone on campus
responses: Lost items are usually turned in to the main security office or the Dean of Students' office. Please check there if you have lost or found something.
There are various clubs including the IT club, Drama club, and spiritual groups like the Christian Union. You can register for any club during the clubs' fair at the start of the semester.

text: how to hire a graduation gown
responses: Graduation gowns are hired after paying the graduation fee. Collection and return dates are announced by the Registrar (ASA) shortly before the graduation ceremony.
After graduation, you become part of the Alumni Association. This network helps you stay connected with the university and provides networking opportunities for your career.

The University Anthem is sung at all official ceremonies and reflects our motto, 'Shaping the Future'. The logo features symbols of knowledge and progress, while our official colors represent Diligence, Excellence, and Integrity.

text: how to cope with loss while in school
I need time off for a funeral
responses: We are deeply sorry for your loss. Please reach out to the Dean of Students to discuss compassionate leave and access grief counseling. Taking care of your mental health during this time is the priority.
Bereavement is a heavy burden. The university offers psychological support to help you navigate this difficult period. Contact the Student Affairs office to book an appointment with a counselor.

text: struggling with being away from home
first time away from my parents
responses: It's completely normal to miss home, especially in your first year. Try to stay connected with family via calls, but also engage in campus activities like the Mentorship Club to build a new community here.
Adjusting to a new environment takes time. Visit the Student Affairs office for tips on managing the transition, or join a student society to meet others who are in the same boat.
Interpersonal conflicts can be distracting. If you're having trouble with a roommate, try a calm conversation first. If it persists, the Dean of Students' office can assist with mediation.
Navigating relationships is part of growth. If a breakup or friendship conflict is affecting your studies, our counselors are available for confidential support.
Burnout is a sign you need a break. Consider talking to your academic advisor or the Dean of Students about taking a short deferment or adjusting your workload.
Don't ignore exhaustion. High-stress periods are common, but persistent lack of motivation should be discussed with a counselor to help you find a healthy balance again.
Timetable Link visit the students portal.
The University Noticeboard has the timetable.
